task_title: Lane_01 CTO review of AIER Code canon/spec draft stack (11 files)
trigger: MSG-L03-L01-REVIEW-20260426-004 (priority high) + Lane_03 packet upload commit af2d9ab
workspace_note: Task spec lists Lane_03 path. Lane_01 operates from its own canonical clone per AMD_LANE01_ROLE_REFRAME §3.1. Same remote, different OS.
top_level_reasoning: Packet has strong coherence and excellent V0/V1 boundary discipline (especially roadmap-spec gates §11-14). NTS authority preserved consistently. 4 specific amendments needed before NTS approval review: (1) version label normalization; (2) memory_spec current_state.md schema reconciliation with shipped v0.1; (3) lane_federation §6.1 internal vendor-name contradiction; (4) unified side-effect taxonomy across capability_matrix + backend_event + productization (currently 3 different 8-class taxonomies). No OBJECT, no SPLIT required.

aier_code_master_canon_v_1_draft.md.reasoning: Comprehensive 40-section foundational canon. Strong: §10 Authority Model preserves NTS, §11-12 V0/V1 scope, §38 Redlines (13), §39 Minimum Canon Invariants (10). Issue: header says 'v0.2 DRAFT' but filename says 'v_1_draft' — labeling drift. Cosmetic but confusing for cold-start readers.

aier_code_master_architecture_v_1_draft..reasoning: 9-layer architecture (Repo Truth → Brain → Memory → Communication → OS Operations → Lane Federation → Runtime V1+ → Backend/Event V1+ → Productization V2+). Excellent §13 system diagram. Clear V0/V1/V2+ boundaries match Master Canon.

aier_code_v_0_architecture_spec_draft.md.reasoning: V0-specific spec; 5 component groups; §17-19 V0/V1/V2+ boundaries match Master Architecture; §18 V0 Gaps registered explicitly (useful executor input).

aier_code_memory_spec_v_1_draft.md.reasoning: Memory layer detail. Strong §5 surfaces, §15 cold-start protocol, §17 authority matrix. CRITICAL ISSUE: §6.2 Required Sections for current_state.md prescribes 15 sections (Phase / Mode / Lanes / Active Task / Open Blockers / NTS Waiting On / Last Verified Commit / Next Allowed Action / etc.), but the SHIPPED runtime/current_state.md (T-RUNTIME-001 + T-L01-RUNTIME-FIX-CHECKLIST-001) has 9 sections (WHO IS WHO / PROJECT MISSION / WHAT SHIPPED / WHAT EXISTS / WHAT IS MISSING / ACTIVE PHASE / HOW TO USE / UPDATE PROTOCOL / ENDPOINT). Same schema-conflict issue I flagged for LAW_N7 §L7.6. Activation creates immediate canon non-conformance with shipped file. Either §6.2 reconciles with shipped v0.1 schema, or T-RUNTIME-002 (queued) lands first to converge.

aier_code_communication_spec_v_1_draft.m.reasoning: 30 sections. Strong: §6 Message Identity matches actual MSG/RSP usage, §15 Arbitration Flow, §17 Authority Matrix. Minor: §8/§9 list conceptual fields but don't reference actual contracts/lane_message.schema.json + lane_response.schema.json — could be tightened but non-blocking.

aier_code_os_operations_spec_v_1_draft.m.reasoning: 24 sections. §5 lists actual OS ops files at correct paths. §10 Amendment Workflow Spec partially overlaps with my v0.3 candidate from prior task (LANE01-CTO-OS-OPERATIONS-REVIEW-AND-UPDATE-V1) — the two should converge in NTS approval phase but neither blocks the other for review.

aier_code_lane_federation_spec_v_1_draft.reasoning: 32 sections. Strong §4 Lane Identity (Lane_<NN> canon, vendor names forbidden), §22 Status Model. INTERNAL CONTRADICTION: §6.1 describes Lane_03 as 'AITAO + Codex / execution + canon-boundary support' which violates own §4.2 forbidden vendor names rule (Codex listed as forbidden canon Lane identity). Either §6.1 description must use role-only ('execution + canon-boundary support') and move 'AITAO + Codex' into LANE_03/lane_laws/LAW_LANE_INFRASTRUCTURE.md per §14, OR §4.2 must amend to allow vendor names as 'implementation note' in role descriptions.

aier_code_capability_matrix_spec_v_1_dra.reasoning: 28 sections. Strong §16 Lane Capability Defaults explicitly cite Lane_01/02/03, §19 Capability Check Protocol. ISSUE: §7 Side-Effect Classes uses 8 classes (0=NO_EFFECT to 7=CRITICAL_CONTROL) but LAW_N10 §L10.5 (still draft per AMD_LANE03_LAWS_N7_N11) uses 6 classes (0=Read-only to 5=Production deploy). Two conflicting taxonomies. Recommend: pick one and have the other reference it. Since LAW_N10 is draft, this spec could become the canonical taxonomy if approved first; or vice versa.

aier_code_backend_event_spec_v_1_draft.m.reasoning: 30 sections. Strong §1-2 forbidden 'AI → API → DB' + lawful 'EVENT → CONTRACT → AIER → ACTION → AUDIT' model, §15 Action Contract Spec, §29 Redlines. ISSUE: §21 Risk Classes (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL — 4 classes) is yet another taxonomy distinct from Capability Matrix §6/§7 + Productization §13 + LAW_N10 §L10.5. Need single unified side-effect/risk taxonomy across canon stack OR explicit cross-reference of how each maps to others.

aier_code_productization_spec_v_1_draft..reasoning: 30 sections. Strong §2 'Product shows truth, does not create truth', §8 Display Truth Rules, §10 Authority Display Rules, §17 Stale Data Rules. §13 UI Action Classes is yet another 8-class taxonomy — third taxonomy in the stack — but internally coherent. Productization is V2+ and not blocking V0/V1 work, so can be approved as-is and reconciled later if NTS chooses.

aier_code_v_0_to_v_1_roadmap_spec_draft..reasoning: 25 sections. Strong §2 'Roadmap is not authority', §11-14 V1 Runtime Gate / Backend Gate / Skill Gate / Productization Gate (10/10/8/7 conditions each — explicit gates). §22 Failure Modes block roadmap from authorizing what canon doesn't. THIS IS THE STRONGEST FILE in the packet — does exactly what the task spec asks for (block premature runtime/backend/productization).

answers_to_7_review_questions.Q1_stack_coherence: MOSTLY YES. Top-down logic clear (canon → architecture → V0 spec → 6 detailed specs → roadmap-spec). Each file has unique role. Dependencies declared in headers. Some duplication between Master Canon and Master Architecture (both define V0 scope) — acceptable redundancy for cold-start readers.
answers_to_7_review_questions.Q2_drift_risks: FOUR identified: (a) hard-coded canonical path D:\UZG\Projects-v2\Uniton_Shared in headers (Lane_03 path; same issue as ACTIVE WORKSPACE_REPO_INTEGRITY_POLICY); (b) Memory Spec §6.2 schema vs shipped current_state.md v0.1 — day-1 non-conformance; (c) Lane Federation §6.1 internal contradiction (vendor names in role description violate own §4.2 rule); (d) 3 different 8-class taxonomies + 1 four-class + LAW_N10's 6-class — five competing side-effect/risk taxonomies in canon stack.
answers_to_7_review_questions.Q3_architecture_correct_for_NTS_review: YES with the 4 amendments. The architecture is internally coherent at the high level. Issues are precise and fixable (not structural).
answers_to_7_review_questions.Q4_V0_V1_boundary_safe: YES, very strong. Roadmap-spec §11-14 explicitly gate runtime/backend/skill/productization. Master Canon §11-12 + Master Architecture §17-19 + V0 Architecture Spec §3-4 all reinforce. Boundary discipline is the strongest part of this packet.
answers_to_7_review_questions.Q5_roadmap_blocks_premature_V1: YES, explicitly. §11 V1 Runtime Gate (10 conditions), §12 V1 Backend Gate (10 conditions), §13 V1 Skill Gate (8 conditions), §14 V1 Productization Gate (7 conditions). §22 Failure Modes block roadmap from authorizing what canon doesn't. §2 'Roadmap follows canon and spec; roadmap does not create canon or bypass spec'.
answers_to_7_review_questions.Q6_split_amend_rename_defer: AMEND 4 files (master_canon version label; memory_spec schema reconcile; lane_federation §6.1 vendor-name fix; capability_matrix + backend_event taxonomy reconcile). DEFER: none. SPLIT: not required (could optionally split side-effect taxonomy into standalone reference doc but not blocking). RENAME: yes — see Q7.
answers_to_7_review_questions.Q7_naming_path_normalization: ISSUES: (a) filenames mix `_v_1_draft.md` vs `_v_0_X_spec_draft.md` — inconsistent ordering of version vs scope; (b) Master Canon header 'v0.2 DRAFT' vs filename 'v_1' — confusing; (c) `v_0`/`v_1` underscored vs `v0`/`v1` un-underscored — pick one convention across all 11; (d) docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/CANON/ already used; Lane_03 also created docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/SHARED/architecture/AIER_CODE_DOCUMENT_STACK_AND_AUTHORITY_MAP_V1.draft.md suggesting eventual SHARED/architecture/ home — pick one; (e) no CANON/README.md or CANON/INDEX.md to orient cold-start readers. RECOMMEND: (1) normalize filenames to single convention (e.g., `aier_code_<scope>_v0.1_draft.md`); (2) decide CANON/ vs SHARED/architecture/ as eventual home; (3) add CANON/README.md (or wherever final home is) listing the 11 files + their roles + dependency order.

blocking_issues: None at top-level — packet can proceed to NTS review with 4 amendments. AMEND verdicts are not OBJECT (not boundary-crossing); they are concrete fixable issues.

Write Lane_01 RSP JSON + readable MD to Lane_03 outbox
